name: Sanguine Sword
+3 ability: Grants Shadow Blade: drains a distant unit's HP with dark power. Knight jobs only.
Additional Notes: VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-14 (Patrick confirmed: works on a Knight, job 76 record 7 -- renders + casts). At +3, grant Gaffgarion's Shadow Blade (ability 165, a ranged dark strike that absorbs the foe's HP) into the wielder's JobCommand menu via the proven Barrage injection. ShadowBlade.cs REUSES every Barrage primitive -- the shipped Barrage module is untouched (its table consts were only widened private->internal). Eligibility WHITELISTED to the Squire/Knight sword sets (records 5/7), primary or secondary, NOT open to every job. RESOLVED 2026-06-14: renders + casts on a Knight (job 76, a normal-executor) -- no blank-name, no special-executor swallow. Ramza's story sword forms ARE mapped 2026-06-15 (Gallant Knight job-byte 3 -> Mettle rec 27; Squire variants 1/2/4 -> 25/26/28, via JobData.JobCommandId / RamzaSwordJobs); verified live casting Shadow Blade from Ramza's Mettle command. Review-hardened: clears the inject ledger on the wielder-gone/unsupported paths so a stale slot index can't stomp a different job's record. Replaces the DRAFT HP-Boost/Regenerate stack.

name: Wrathblade
+3 ability: Cheats death once per battle, rising unseen at low HP.
Additional Notes: VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-14 (one clean end-to-end run; multi-fight reliability unconfirmed). At +3, a lethal hit on the wielder flops it as a corpse — Invisible held (band +0x47/0x10, re-stamped; it breaks the instant the unit acts) so single-target AI ignores it — for 2 of its own turns, then the engine's OWN Reraise (+0x47/0x20) stands it up animated at ~10% HP, once per battle. SYNERGY: reviving at ~10% = ~90% missing HP = near-max Wrathblade (missing-HP, formula 67) damage. Lifecycle Watching/Possum/Finish/Recover (pure FeignDeath.Policy.cs::Step; FeignDeath.cs reads facts -> Step -> guarded writes). Possum turns counted off the active-unit struct (TurnQueue 0x14077D2A0) — the band CT byte +0x25 read too noisy to count. The finishing blow strikes ONLY when the wielder is UP NEXT (!ActiveUnitIsWielder && +0x25 >= FeignUpNextCt 50), keeping the dead-and-scheduled window a sliver: killing far from its turn CRASHED the engine, mid-CT the engine UNDID the death, at the active turn it STUCK dead-but-active. Recover holds the dead/KO bit (+0x45/0x20) CLEARED ~3 s so the stand-up leaves no hearts. Safety: degrades to survive-at-1-HP when the wielder is the last unit standing (no party wipe); re-arms on battle restart / full heal. SetHP funcs Denuvo-sealed — the held Reraise bit is the only lever. p3Desc: 'Plays dead at a lethal blow, 1/battle: ignored by foes 2 turns, then springs back low HP.'

name: Swiftedge
+3 ability: Builds Speed +1 each turn the wielder acts, up to +5. A hit resets it.
Additional Notes: VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-14 (Patrick confirmed: Swiftedge damage + Speed climbed per ACTED turn, log-confirmed `afterimage: ... Speed 13->16 (stacks 1/5)`; 10 xUnit tests). At +3 the wielder's Speed ramps +1 per completed turn (Tuning.AfterimageSpeedPerTurn), capped at +5 (AfterimageSpeedCap), and resets to 0 on any hit. Swiftedge's damage is Speed x WP (formula 99), so the ramp accelerates its damage -- keep acting unharmed to blur faster. Runtime: GrowthEngine owns Swiftedge's speed lane (Route yields it), captures natural + re-applies the ramped target against the per-turn normalize (HoldTimedStat idiom); turns counted off TurnTracker, the hit-reset off a ReadHp drop. LIVE-VERIFY the speed write actually moves damage (the GrowthEngine all-party live-test-pending caveat). Replaces the DRAFT Swiftspell/Speed-Surge stack (reaction/support slots were invalid).

name: Arcanum
+3 ability: Steals one buff from a struck foe for the wielder to wear 3 turns.
Additional Notes: BUILT 2026-06-14 (logic + runtime + tests; PENDING LIVE VERIFY). At +3, a hit STEALS the struck foe's highest-priority holdable buff: stripped from the foe, held on the wielder for larcenyTurns (3) of the wielder's own turns (counted via TurnTracker -- the player band CT reads flat 0), then dropped. Maim-shaped latch (HP-diff in the acted window, enemy-fingerprint filtered) + wielder-side hold (Wielder.Locate). Safety: only grants+holds if the wielder LACKS the buff (expiry never strips the wielder's own); level-drift tolerant (Band.LevelMatchesRoster). The transfer is test-proven on the holdable Reraise/Invisible bits (+0x47). COVERAGE LIVE-PENDING: the marquee buffs (Haste/Protect/Shell/Reflect/Regen/Float) are UNMAPPED -- map them with poison_probe.py diff+holdbit and add rows to LarcenyPolicy.Stealable. Replaces the DRAFT Magick-Boost/Reflexes stack.

name: Materia Blade
Additional Notes: Materia Blade is the only living weapon on a unique (non-PA%) growth curve, mimicking FF7: power scales with the wielder's CURRENT HP (hardest at full life, weakest when badly hurt) and the whole curve rises with kill tier. Base WP is +1 over vanilla. Damage scaling by tier then current-HP band [100% / 75-99% / 50-74% / 25-49% / <25%]: +0 = 115/110/80/70/50; +1 (5 kills) = 120/113/83/73/53; +2 (25) = 125/116/86/76/56; +3 (50) = 130/120/90/80/60.

name: Ame-no-Murakumo
+3 ability: Makes the wielder act first at battle start (the quick draw).
Additional Notes: Iai (at battle start): sets the wielder's Speed to the highest Speed in the battle + 1 so they act first (the quick draw). If two units both carry a +3 Ame-no-Murakumo, the game decides which one goes first.

name: Kiyomori

name: Kiku-ichimonji
+3 ability: Standing perfectly still arms the next strike to hit devastatingly hard.
Additional Notes: Mushin STACKING (Kiku +3): each full-wait turn (no move, no action) banks 1 stack, max MushinMaxStacks=3; the next attack spends every banked stack in one boosted hit, then resets to 0. Held PA = round(natural x (1 + factor)), factor = tier-3 growth 0.30 + MushinBonus 0.75 per stack (additive). So 1/2/3 stacks = x2.05 / x2.8 / x3.55 PA (about 1.6x / 2.15x / 2.7x a normal +3 swing). Constants in Tuning.cs, math in Mushin.Policy.cs PaHeld. Stacking live; consume rebuild pending (LW-4).

name: Hushward Rod
Additional Notes: SHELVED 2026-06-10 (code dormant, config removed): SIGNATURE "Unbroken Chant" (support grant, Signatures/GrowthEngine HoldSignature path) — SHIPPED 2026-06-10: at +3 the wielder gains Swiftspell (support 226, +0x98 byte 3 mask 0x08), OR-set + held each tick; stacks with the player's own support pick; released (AND-cleared) if the rod leaves the wielder's hands mid-battle, never stripping a player-picked Swiftspell. name: Umbral Rod
+3 ability: Restores HP and MP on each move.
Additional Notes: TRIGGER HISTORY (4 failures): (1) Global TurnTracker acted-edge -- cursor-following struct mis-credited edges. (2) Band +0x25 ACtSlam CT -- write-only, zero transitions observed live. (3) Band +0x09 ACtTurn CT -- never reached >=90 across full player turns. (4) Actor latch rising edge -- fired for action 1, then the second player action produced NO latch line, NO turn line -- global Acted flag pulses unreliably (documented trap). CURRENT TRIGGER: position-poll MoveWatch (SpiritualFont.Policy.cs). Each tick (inLive, active, located): read gx/gy from the first located band entry. First sighting baselines silently. New tile must be stable 3 consecutive ticks (filters animation flicker and (0,0)-band pulses). Rate-capped 90 ticks (~3 s) after each fire. Move-only turns NOW PAY (old gap closed). Knockback/teleport also pays (intended). On fire: HP += round(maxHP*10%); MP += round(maxMP*10%). Band +0x18/+0x1A LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-10. Per-battle layout validation retained as sanity guard. SHELVED on this row: Life Sap (LifeSap.cs) dormant; see branch shelved-rod-signatures.

name: Dragon Rod
Additional Notes: SHELVED 2026-06-10 (code dormant, config removed): SIGNATURE "Wyrmblood" (customRuntime, Wyrmblood.cs) — SHIPPED 2026-06-10: at +3, at each wielder COMPLETED-turn edge (TurnTracker rising-Acted: the heal lands as the wielder finishes acting, adjacency from the post-move tile — not pre-move like vanilla Regen), the wielder AND every ALLY within 1 Manhattan tile heal their OWN maxHP/8 (Tuning.WyrmbloodDiv = vanilla Regen rate), clamped at full. EMULATED regen — the Regen status bit is unmapped, never touched; guarded HP writes on the authoritative band entries. Allies positively identified (static-array player-slot fingerprints — never enemies); dead never healed; one heal per fingerprint per splash. Each splash dev-logged. p3Desc: 'The wielder regenerates each turn, and the warmth mends adjacent allies.' Name 'Dragonheart' FORBIDDEN (Dragoon reaction collision). Replaces the DRAFT Regenerate stack with the same fantasy in buildable form.

name: Rod of Faith
+3 ability: Grants Master Teleportation while the wielder is below a third HP.
Additional Notes: REWORKED 2026-06-10: the 3-turn cap is RETIRED (the band CT it read, +0x25 = ExtraTurn's WRITE target, never ticked live; recovery release player-verified same session). Now: when the +3 wielder's HP drops below 30% of max (Tuning.RaptureHpPct), Master Teleportation (movement 243 = ability.en key 499; Tuning.RaptureMoveId) REPLACES the movement field (band +0x80, 3 bytes) and is HELD UNTIL THE WIELDER RECOVERS to/above the threshold — no turn count. Recovery IS the hysteresis: the next drop below threshold arms a fresh window. Maim's save-once/hold/restore discipline + never-re-save invariant intact. Restores on unequip/tier loss/wielder death (3-tick streak)/battle exit. DUAL-WIELD: no other movement-bit writer exists (the Wellspring font bits were retired for runtime writes), so the held image carries only the teleport bit. MOVEMENT 243 CONFIRMED LIVE 2026-06-10: player teleported; FULL SIGNATURE PLAYER-VERIFIED ('works flawlessly') same session; arm-time read-back (SET/MISS) still logged; fallback: flip RaptureMoveId to 242. p3Desc: 'Below a third health, the wielder gains Master Teleportation until they recover.' The glass cannon's escape hatch — holds as long as the crisis holds.

Removing the granted Dual Wield unequips the off-hand gun, so it must stay on.

name: Eclipsebolt
+3 ability: Shortens Doom's countdown to 1 turn instead of 3.
Additional Notes: SHIPPED (EagleEye.cs + EagleEye.Policy.cs, verified live). base applies Doom at ~25% (formula 1; reverted from the 100% test config). At +3, every enemy Doom's countdown is forced to 1 (band +0x59) so the mark resolves on the victim's next turn — the gamble payoff (Eagle Eye only fires on the ~25% of hits that land Doom). PROVEN LIVE 2026-06-09: Doom bit = band +0x49 mask 0x01, countdown = +0x59 (init 3, -1 per victim turn, death at 0); wrote 1 mid-battle -> head-counter showed 1 LIVE (no cosmetic seam) -> victim died next turn (clean engine death, Dead +0x45/0x20). Undead no-op the expiry. Aura model (matches CharmLock): hastens any enemy Doom while equipped, not just the wielder's

name: Huntress
